acceptance_targets: [chapter_09_f003] PASS if Beau's revised dialogue in spans 15-19 and 119-126 uses contractions in at least 3 of 4 eligible clauses with no more than one intentional full-form emphasis; FAIL if multiple default uncontracted forms remain without dramatic justification.

[chapter_09_f004] PASS if each of the two argument spans contains one unmistakably Lena-only move (procedure chain then hard burst) and one unmistakably Beau-only move (idiomatic side-road or unfinished-clause stress tell), and at least two lines are no longer attribution-swappable; FAIL if both speakers still rely on near-identical polished one-liner cadence.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Beau's revised dialogue in spans 15-19 and 119-126 uses contractions in at least 3 of 4 eligible clauses with no more than one intentional full-form emphasis; FAIL if multiple default uncontracted forms remain without dramatic justification.
findings.evidence: Beau is marked high-contraction in style rules but defaults to uncontracted forms in pressure dialogue: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:15 ("We are"); snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:125 ("does not").
findings.problem: Register drift toward formal legal diction flattens Beau's locally idiomatic voice under stress.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Beau's lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:119, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:125 to contracted, spoken rhythm unless deliberate emphasis is explicitly signaled.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each of the two argument spans contains one unmistakably Lena-only move (procedure chain then hard burst) and one unmistakably Beau-only move (idiomatic side-road or unfinished-clause stress tell), and at least two lines are no longer attribution-swappable; FAIL if both speakers still rely on near-identical polished one-liner cadence.
findings.evidence: Lena-Beau conflict exchanges converge on similar aphoristic cadence and syntax: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:123; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:125; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:295; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:325.
findings.problem: Idiolect separation weakens in key arguments; speaker lines become partially swappable instead of clearly profile-anchored.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the two argument spans at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:317: give Lena a procedural over-explain beat that snaps into hard short declaratives, and give Beau one sidelong anecdotal or self-mocking lead-in with an unfinished clause before his practical conclusion.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
